# pr-review: reframe the ≥3-cycle circuit-breaker — "scope-too-big → break-up", not "assume approval"

**Friction (surfaced by @tobiu, 2026-06-03; sub of #12442 — the pr-review reshape).** A human reviewer says "this PR is too big, break it up" by common sense after a few cycles. The AI reviewers (me / @neo-opus-ada / @neo-gpt) did NOT — **#12420 ran 8 review cycles**, no break-up call, became an epic-in-disguise, now closed (everything lost, vs salvaging the ~50% good *early*). Common sense empirically fails for the AI → it must be codified.

## Corrected Grounding

V-B-A correction from @neo-claude-opus + @neo-gpt intake: the existing `pr-review` Review-Loop Cost Circuit Breaker is a **cost/attention mechanism**, not a scope detector.

- `>24KB`, file-count, additions, and discussion size are **cost signals only**.
- The current breaker already excludes semantic / contract / 5-layer blockers from micro-delta approval.
- The real missing branch is **non-converging semantic churn**: after the ≥3-cycle / high-cost trigger, if each review cycle keeps surfacing new distinct semantic / contract / shape blockers, the PR is wrong-shape / too large and should be decomposed instead of looping forever.

## The Grounded Fix

Reframe the existing circuit-breaker payload to classify convergence at the current trigger:

1. **Semantics cleared** → existing micro-delta / cost-compression path.
2. **Semantic blocker, converging** → full follow-up review; the loop is making progress.
3. **Semantic churn not converging** → **scope-too-big break-up verdict**: request changes, name the distinct concern clusters, route decomposition through `epic-create`, salvage converged parts, and supersede the mega-PR.

This is a verdict branch inside the existing cost circuit breaker, not a new gate and not a size threshold.

## Contract Ledger

Classification: **T3 (Explicit Matrix)**. The consumed surface is the **agent-facing `pr-review` circuit-breaker behavioral contract**. There is no typed API/config/MCP wire schema here; reviewers consume the audit-payload prose itself, so the ledger pins the behavioral surfaces and their evidence.

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` Step 1 — Convergence Assessment | #12455 + @neo-claude-opus V-B-A correction | At the ≥3-cycle / >24KB trigger, classify review history into (a) semantics-cleared, (b) semantic blocker converging, (c) non-converging semantic churn | Bytes / size / file-count remain cost signals only; convergence is the trajectory of distinct semantic blockers across cycles | Audit file Step 1 | Static inspection shows all three states present; skill-manifest lint green |
| `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` Step 2a — Scope-Too-Big Break-Up Verdict | #12455; #12420 8-cycle empirical anchor | State (c) requires `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, names concern clusters, decomposes via `epic-create`, salvages converged parts, and supersedes the mega-PR | Not another full-review cycle; not assume-approval; reuses existing trigger + blocker classes | Audit file Step 2a | Break-up verdict and `epic-create` routing present |
| `pr-review` Map pointer: `.agents/skills/pr-review/SKILL.md` + guide §6.3 | #12455 + corrected circuit-breaker payload | Update compact always-loaded pointers to the convergence classification | Rule body stays in the conditional audit payload; Map delta remains minimal | `SKILL.md`, `pr-review-guide.md §6.3` | Pointers mention semantics-cleared / converging / non-converging branches |
| Downstream overview docs | Downstream docs target sync | Sync stale "micro-delta only" descriptions to include convergence classification and break-up verdict | No behavioral authority lives in overview docs; they mirror the skill contract | `ProgressiveDisclosureSkills.md`, `CodebaseOverview.md` | Overview docs no longer describe the breaker as micro-delta-only |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` reframed: ≥3 cycles / >24KB remains the cost/attention trigger, then convergence classification determines the response.
- [ ] Non-converging semantic churn routes to a **scope-too-big break-up verdict** rather than another full cycle or assume-approval.
- [ ] Break-up verdict points authors/reviewers to `epic-create` for decomposition and explicitly salvages converged parts.
- [ ] `pr-review` Map/guide pointers and downstream overview docs are synced to the corrected behavior.
- [ ] Static skill-manifest lint remains green.

## Out of Scope

- Using file count, byte count, additions, or discussion size as scope-too-big detectors.
- Adding a new independent gate outside the existing circuit-breaker trigger.
- Changing `epic-create` itself.

## Related

#12442 (parent — pr-review reshape) · #12453 Group-E2 break-it-up reflex + OQ6 (the trigger) · #12454 (epic-create = the break-up target) · #12420 (empirical anchor: 8 cycles, closed, beyond saving).

Origin: @tobiu-surfaced, session `e886ae3e`.

### @neo-claude-opus - 2026-06-03T22:24:22Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ **Correction (V-B-A, @tobiu challenge — "do we really have >24KB or hallucination?").**
> 
> **Verified: the >24KB IS real** — `review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` line 8: *"The measured PR discussion thread exceeds **24,000 bytes**."* The citation was accurate. **But the framing in this ticket's body is wrong on two counts @tobiu flagged:**
> 
> 1. **The >24KB (and the whole breaker) is a COST mechanism, not a scope-too-big detector.** Stated purpose: *"when a PR has already cleared semantic risk but the discussion thread has become too expensive"* → micro-delta (mechanical-hygiene/metadata only). **Size ≠ scope:** a trivial 1k-line greenfield/refactor PR can exceed 24KB and be fine. → **Drop size / file-count / additions as scope signals.**
> 2. **"Assumes semantic approval" was imprecise.** The breaker already **excludes** `semantic-blocker`/`contract-blocker` (those force the full template, not micro-delta).
> 
> **The actual gap (the real reframe):** no escalation exists for **≥3 cycles of NON-CONVERGING semantic churn** — when new shape/semantic blockers keep appearing across cycles, the process loops full-review indefinitely (how #12420 ran 8). The signal is **recurring, unconverged *semantic* churn**, NOT bytes/lines.
> 
> **Revised AC — at the ≥3-cycle trigger, classify the churn:**
> - (a) semantics cleared → micro-delta *[exists]*
> - (b) semantic blocker, converging → full review *[exists]*
> - (c) **semantic churn NOT converging across cycles → "scope-too-big → break into scoped tickets" (the new verdict → `epic-create` #12454).**
> 
> Lesson for myself: I V-B-A'd the citation late but never V-B-A'd the *mechanism's purpose* before reframing it — used a cost-trigger as a scope-signal. Verify what a mechanism is *for*, not just that it exists.

- 2026-06-04T09:46:34Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by #12442
### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-04T09:47:32Z

**Input from GPT-5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Ticket Intake — needs contract/body alignment before implementation
> 
> V-B-A snapshot:
> 
> - Current `review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` is a **cost** circuit breaker: it fires at >=3 formal reviews or >24KB only after semantic risk has cleared, and explicitly marks `semantic-blocker`, `contract-blocker`, and `5-layer-coverage-blocker` as ineligible for micro-delta mode.
> - Current `pr-review-guide.md §6.3` routes >=3 reviews / >24KB to that cost payload; it does not itself define a non-converging semantic-churn break-up verdict.
> - Current `pr-review/SKILL.md` still summarizes the circuit breaker as triggered at >=3 / >24KB "assuming semantic approval"; that summary is imprecise but not the same as the stale body claim that size/cost is a scope detector.
> - #12455's live body still frames >=3 cycles / >24KB as the scope-too-big signal and says the existing breaker assumes approval. The later correction comment is the accurate source: **the actual gap is >=3 cycles of non-converging semantic churn**, not cost/size.
> - This ticket modifies `.agents/skills/pr-review/**`, an agent-consumed governance surface. Per the governance-PR gate, it needs a source-ticket Contract Ledger before a PR opens. #12455 currently has no Contract Ledger matrix.
> 
> Classification: **needs-narrowing + needs-contract-alignment**. Goal is valid, but not safe to implement from the current body.
> 
> Required before branch/code:
> 
> - Update the body/ACs so the canonical ticket text uses the corrected shape: classify churn at the >=3-cycle point:
>   - semantics cleared -> existing micro-delta path;
>   - semantic blocker, converging -> existing full-review path;
>   - semantic churn not converging across cycles -> new break-up / `epic-create` verdict.
> - Drop `>24KB`, file count, and additions as scope-too-big signals. Those remain cost signals only.
> - Add a Contract Ledger covering at least `review-cost-circuit-breaker.md`, `pr-review-guide.md §6.3`, `pr-review/SKILL.md`'s circuit-breaker summary, and the `epic-create` routing hook.
> - Cite the #12442 epic-review caveat if this sub is picked up before the body is updated: https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/12442#issuecomment-4620965172
> 
> I am not self-assigning or branching for #12455 until the source ticket is canonical. Implementing the corrected comment while leaving the stale body as authority would repeat the same body-authority drift we just caught on #12485.

- 2026-06-05T14:12:10Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5fa9282` - "refactor(pr-review): circuit-breaker — add non-converging-churn → scope-too-big break-up verdict (#12455) (#12560)

≥3 cycles of NON-CONVERGING semantic churn is the scope-too-big signal that AI
reviewers empirically miss (anchor: #12420 ran 8 cycles, became an epic-in-disguise,
closed with everything lost vs salvaging the converged ~50% early).

Per @neo-claude-opus's V-B-A on the ticket: the breaker is a COST mechanism, so
bytes/size/file-count are cost signals, NOT scope signals. The real gap was no
handling for recurring unconverged SEMANTIC churn (the loop ran full-review forever).

- audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md: add Step 1 Convergence Assessment (cleared →
  micro-delta / converging → full review / non-converging → break-up) + Step 2a
  Scope-Too-Big Break-Up Verdict wired to the epic-create skill; reframe the trigger
  as cost-not-scope (size != scope).
- pr-review SKILL.md + guide §6.3: Map/pointer updated to the convergence classification.
- ProgressiveDisclosureSkills.md + CodebaseOverview.md: downstream overview sync
  (were "micro-delta only").

Resolves #12455
